rationale_for_baseline_tier: Post-antibiotic oral thrush is benign and treated with a topical antifungal at home, with routine review (T1). The pain-limited intake in a patient living alone adds a dehydration safety-net to ED (T4); oesophageal extension routes to ED for systemic antifungal (T4); unexplained/recurrent disease routes to GP work-up.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Deep pain in the centre of the chest on swallowing, or a sense of food sticking on the way down
escalation_edges.rationale: Oesophageal candidiasis needs systemic antifungal and assessment for immunosuppression.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get deep pain in the centre of your chest when swallowing, or food feels like it's sticking on the way down — go to the Emergency Department / see a doctor urgently.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to drink despite pain relief, not passing urine, or marked lightheadedness
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't drink even with pain relief, stop passing urine, or feel very lightheaded — go to the Emergency Department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower threshold for ED if rehydration fails; give explicit dehydration red flags.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Use the antifungal mouthwash four times a day (don't eat/drink for 30 minutes after), take pain relief before drinking, and keep sipping cool bland fluids. It should clear over about a week.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Topical antifungal + GP review in 5–7 days; ED for oesophageal features or failed rehydration
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED for deep odynophagia or if you can't drink at all.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Post-antibiotic pseudomembranous oral candidiasis; oesophageal extension screened negative; nystatin; consider predisposing-factor work-up if recurrent/unexplained; dehydration safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating thrush with no oesophageal or dehydration safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Oesophageal candidiasis (and possible immunodeficiency) is missed, or the patient becomes dehydrated.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not considering underlying immunosuppression when there is no clear cause.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for simple post-antibiotic oral thrush that can drink — unnecessary.
